figures.caption: Figure 1. UHN settings: (a) single-model, UHN H generates a single base model f ; (b) multi-model, UHN H generates a set of base models {fi} within one model family; (c) multi-task, UHN H generates base models f, g, q, . . . across potentially heterogeneous architectures for different tasks; and (d) recursive generation, in which the root UHN H0 generates an intermediate UHN H1, which then generates the base model f .
Table 15. Direct-training best hyperparameters selected by grid search (universality experiment).
Table 14. Direct-training hyperparameter sweep grids (universality experiment). Table entries list the sweep-grid values for ηtrain and Etrain.
Table 8. Local structure descriptor layout sℓ,j ∈ R21.
Table 23. Index-encoding ablation results. #Params denotes the number of trainable parameters of UHN; Acc. denotes test accuracy.

Table 1. Single-model universality results for classification tasks (image/graph/text). #Params denotes the number of trainable parameters; Acc. denotes test accuracy. Due to space constraints, formula-regression results are reported in Table 11.

Figure 5. Multi-model Transformer Mixed epoch-averaged training loss with and without the initialization stage (“w/ init” and “w/o init”; log-scale y-axis). Loss is averaged over all training samples within each epoch.
Table 6. Index descriptor layout vi ∈ R10.

Table 11. Single-model universality results for formula regression on the 15 special functions following the KAN benchmark (Liu et al., 2024). Model (#Params) reports the base model architecture and its trainable parameter count; the trainable parameter count of UHN is fixed at 612,117 for all functions. RMSE denotes test root-mean-squared error (lower is better).

Table 5. Recursive-task results on MNIST. “Direct training” and “UHN (no recursion)” are taken from the single-model MNIST MLP experiment (Section 4.2.1). “UHN rec.” reports the accuracy of the leaf MLP generated via a recursion chain of depth K. Acc. denotes test accuracy.
Table 13. UHN best hyperparameters selected by grid search (universality experiment).
Figure 4. Visualization of the third convolution-layer kernels of the generated base model.
